tdeeWomen.title: TDEE Calculator for Women · Macro Log
tdeeWomen.heading: tdee calculator for women
tdeeWomen.body: Estimate your daily calorie burn (TDEE) and macro targets. Tuned for the same Mifflin-St Jeor formula doctors and dietitians use, with sensible protein defaults for women cutting, bulking, or maintaining.

tdeeMen.title: TDEE Calculator for Men · Macro Log
tdeeMen.heading: tdee calculator for men
tdeeMen.body: Estimate your daily calorie burn (TDEE) and macro targets. Same Mifflin-St Jeor formula clinicians use, with protein defaults tuned for men cutting, bulking, or holding lean mass.

cutting.title: Cutting Calculator · Calorie Deficit & Protein · Macro Log
cutting.heading: cutting calculator
cutting.body: Calorie deficit + high protein for fat loss while preserving muscle. Targets ~11 kcal/lb and 1g/lb protein — the same defaults strength coaches recommend for sustainable cutting phases.

bulking.title: Bulking Calculator · Lean Surplus · Macro Log
bulking.heading: bulking calculator
bulking.body: Lean surplus targets for clean muscle growth: ~17 kcal/lb and 0.8g/lb protein. Aggressive enough to build, conservative enough to keep fat gain in check.

maintenance.title: Maintenance Calorie Calculator · Macro Log
maintenance.heading: maintenance calorie calculator
maintenance.body: Find the calorie target that holds your current weight. Useful between cuts and bulks, or as a baseline before deciding which direction to go.

keto.title: Keto Macro Calculator · Macro Log
keto.heading: keto macro calculator
keto.body: Keto-friendly calorie + protein targets. Macro Log doesn't track carbs by default — once you're in ketosis the lever that matters is calories in vs out, with adequate protein to keep muscle. Use this to set your baseline.

weightLoss.title: Weight Loss Calculator · Macro Log
weightLoss.heading: weight loss calculator
weightLoss.body: How many calories should you eat to lose weight? Enter your current weight and we'll estimate your daily kcal + protein target for sustainable fat loss — no crash diet, no zero-carb gimmick.

protein.title: Protein Calculator · Daily g per Pound · Macro Log
protein.heading: protein calculator
protein.body: How much protein do you need? The research-backed range is 0.7–1g per pound of bodyweight, depending on whether you're cutting, maintaining, or bulking. Enter your weight to see your target.

transformations.subtitle: Every card below is a real Macro Log user who chose to make their progress public. Click any to see their full transformation page.
transformations.error: Couldn't load transformations. Try again later.
transformations.empty: No public profiles yet — be the first.
transformations.ctaBody: Free calorie + protein tracker with built-in TDEE calculator.

publicProfile.notFoundBody: This profile doesn't exist or has been made private.
publicProfile.errorBody: Couldn't load this profile. Try again later.
publicProfile.ctaBody: Free calorie + protein tracker with built-in TDEE calculator.

publicProfileSettings.title: Public profile
publicProfileSettings.body: Share a transformation page at macrolog.app/u/your-slug. Off by default.
publicProfileSettings.slugHelp: 3–30 chars, lowercase a–z, 0–9, hyphens. Must be unique.
publicProfileSettings.errorFormat: Slug must be 3–30 chars, lowercase a–z 0–9 -.

landing.heroMockupAlt: Today screen: kcal ring at 1,553 of 2,040, protein ring at 138g, carbs and fat chips, recent entry preview.
landing.heroSub: a quiet, private log for the one question that matters. calories, protein, weight, fasting — all in one place, with an AI coach that actually reads your data.
landing.proofCaptureBody: photo → macros. barcode lookup. meal presets. apple shortcuts webhook. four ways in, one ledger out.
landing.proofTdeeBody: fourteen days in, the app calibrates a measured TDEE from your own weight + intake trend — not a generic Mifflin-St Jeor guess.
landing.proofCoachBody: ask the coach why the scale stalled, whether to adjust your target, what the red flags are. grounded in your actual data — no generic advice.
landing.privacyBody: your body data stays yours. we don't sell it, don't serve ads, don't train AI on it. photos sent for analysis are processed ephemerally and not retained. export or delete everything at any time.
landing.freeBody: the full logging loop, forever. everything you need to answer "how many calories do I have left?" every day.
landing.freeF1: unlimited manual entries, weight, fasting, measurements
landing.freeF2: 3 photo analyses / day · 3 AI coach questions / day
landing.proBody: for people who log seriously. removes all the quotas and unlocks power-user capture.
landing.proF3: weekly AI report (cached, grounded in your data)
landing.proF5: pro themes, priority support, 7-day free trial
landing.pricingFinePrint: cancel anytime from the customer portal. stripe handles billing; we never see your card.
landing.quickTargetsLead: Common starting points by current weight. Each opens a public page with the numbers — no sign-up required.
landing.comparisonsLead: Honest, side-by-side comparisons against the alternatives — including where they win.

app.subtitle: a rolling fourteen-day record of weight, intake, and expenditure.
app.openingErrorBody: The server didn't respond in time. This usually clears on a retry.

update.title: Update Available
update.body: A new version of Macro Log is ready. Reload to get the latest features and fixes.

dashboard.tdeeHelpTitle: Estimated calories you burn on an average day. After 14 days of logging this shifts from the formula estimate to your real measured rate.

onboarding.blurb: six quick answers, about a minute. everything here is used only to estimate your daily calorie target — you can change any of it later.
onboarding.notMedicalAdvice: not medical advice — these numbers are estimates, not a prescription. talk to a doctor or RD before making clinical changes.
onboarding.stepIdentityBody: start with the inputs the calorie formula needs most: your height, age, and biological sex.
onboarding.stepActivityBody: pick the option that best matches your usual week. this only seeds your starting estimate — real logging will refine it.
onboarding.stepTargetBody: choose the pace you want and, if you have one, add a goal weight. you can change both later.
onboarding.ageGateLabel: I confirm I'm 13 or older (16+ if I reside in the EU).
onboarding.ageGateCaption: required by COPPA + EU data-protection law — we don't knowingly collect data from minors below this age.
onboarding.errorAgeGate: please confirm your age to continue.
onboarding.sexCaption: required by the mifflin-st jeor formula to estimate your baseline metabolic rate.
onboarding.activitySedentaryBlurb: desk job, little or no exercise
onboarding.paceCaption: pick maintain if you just want to track. a cut pace subtracts calories from your tdee.
onboarding.errorRequiredSex: pick a sex option to continue.
onboarding.errorRequiredActivity: pick an activity level to continue.
onboarding.errorRequiredPace: pick a weekly target to continue.

status.subtitle: live health of macro log's backing services.
status.fetchError: couldn't reach firestore. the app itself may be degraded.

summary.healthy: the scheduler, firestore, and hosting are all responding within normal windows.
summary.degraded: the scheduled heartbeat is running late. the app still works; some server-side jobs may be delayed.
summary.down: the scheduled heartbeat is stale or unreachable. server-side jobs (weekly reports, reminders) are likely paused until this clears.

history.freeWindow: Free tier shows the last 90 days. Upgrade to Pro for unlimited history — same window the charts already use.

privacy.lastUpdated: last updated 2026-04-12 · plain english, no dark patterns.
privacy.storeBody1: When you sign in with Google, we store your email, display name, and profile photo URL — nothing else from your Google account. Everything else in Macro Log is stuff you enter yourself: weight, calories, protein, dates, body measurements, meal labels, training toggles, and custom meal presets.
privacy.storeBody2: All of this is stored in Google Cloud Firestore, scoped to your user ID. No one else — including us — can read it without your Google login.
privacy.geminiHeading: What we send to Google's Gemini AI
privacy.geminiIntro: Two features send data to Google's Gemini AI:
privacy.geminiPhoto: Photo-to-Macros: the meal photo you take is sent to Gemini 2.0 Flash for calorie + protein estimation. Photos are processed in-flight and not retained by us. Google's API retention policy applies — see Gemini API terms.
privacy.geminiCoach: AI Coach consultations: your question, 14-day log summary, profile fields (age, sex, height, activity level, pace), and current TDEE are sent to Gemini as context for each consultation. Your raw email, name, or photo are never included.
privacy.dontSell: We don't sell your data to anyone.
privacy.dontTrain: We don't use your data to train any AI model.
privacy.dontShare: We don't share your data with anyone outside our list of sub-processors: Google Cloud / Firebase (hosting, auth, Firestore, FCM), Google Gemini API (photo analysis + AI coach), Stripe (payments only — if you subscribe), and Sentry (crash reports, scrubbed of personal content). We don't use analytics trackers and don't run ads.
privacy.callExport: Export: download all your logs as CSV from the dashboard at any time.
privacy.callFullExport: Full JSON export: every profile field, log, preset, weight, measurement, report, and quota record we hold for you — portable format for GDPR Art. 20 requests.
privacy.callDelete: Delete: use the button below to permanently erase your account and every log, preset, weight, measurement, and report tied to it. This is irreversible.
privacy.gdprBody: If you live in the European Economic Area, the UK, or Switzerland, the General Data Protection Regulation applies to your data. You can exercise the following rights at any time — email us or use the in-app controls described in "what you can do" above.
privacy.gdprAccess: Access — ask for a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
privacy.gdprRectify: Rectification — correct any inaccurate or incomplete data through the app or by emailing us.
privacy.gdprErase: Erasure — delete your account and all associated data (Privacy → Delete account).
privacy.gdprPortable: Portability — export your logs as CSV from Dashboard → Export.
privacy.gdprRestrict: Restriction — ask us to stop processing your data while a dispute is resolved.
privacy.gdprObject: Objection — object to any processing you believe isn't legitimate.
privacy.ccpaBody: If you live in California, the CCPA and CPRA apply. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioural advertising, full stop. You have the right to know what personal data we hold, to request deletion, and to appeal any denial. Use the same export / delete controls above, or email us directly.
privacy.jurisdictionBody: The data controller for Macro Log is the product owner. Preferred contact for data-subject requests is email: gabrielandresbermudez@gmail.com. If you can't use email, open a public issue at our GitHub issue tracker or use the in-app Settings → Feedback button. We respond within 30 days as required by GDPR. This policy and your use of Macro Log are governed by the laws of the operator's home jurisdiction (Puerto Rico, United States), without regard to conflict-of-law principles.
privacy.medicalBody: Macro Log is not a medical device, and the AI coach is not a dietician. Nothing in the app is medical advice. If you're doing anything clinically meaningful — a cut below 1,500 kcal, weight loss of more than 1% body weight per week, or eating for a medical condition — talk to a real human doctor or RD first.
privacy.signInFirst: sign in first to delete your account.
privacy.confirmEmail: Permanently erase {{email}} and every log, preset, weight, measurement, and report tied to it. This cannot be undone.

terms.lastUpdated: last updated 2026-04-12 · short, because you shouldn't need a lawyer to read this.
terms.dealBody: Macro Log is a calorie, protein, and weight tracker with an AI coach. By signing in, you agree to use it as intended — a personal logging tool — and not to abuse it (scraping, reverse-engineering the webhook, attempting to access other users' data, etc).
terms.medBody: The numbers this app shows (TDEE, target, envelope, coach replies) are estimates, not prescriptions. The AI coach is a language model trained on public text — treat it like a knowledgeable friend who could be wrong, not a doctor or dietitian. Talk to a real human professional before making significant dietary changes, especially if you have a medical condition, are pregnant, under 18, or recovering from an eating disorder.
terms.dataBody: You own your data. You can export it as CSV or delete your account at any time from the privacy page. We keep backups for up to 30 days as part of standard Google Cloud Firestore operations; these are not human-accessible and are auto-purged.
terms.availBody: Macro Log runs on Google Cloud. We do our best to keep it up, but don't guarantee uptime. We may change features, pricing, or these terms — when we do, the footer's "last updated" date will change and meaningful changes will be announced in-app.
terms.liabilityBody: Macro Log is provided "as is." We're not liable for decisions you make based on its output, for lost data, or for any indirect harm from using the app. To the maximum extent allowed by law, our total liability to you is capped at whatever you've paid us in the last 12 months (which for most users is zero).
terms.subsBody: Pro is offered monthly ($3/mo) or annual ($24/yr) via Stripe. A 7-day free trial comes with every first subscription. Plans auto-renew at the end of each billing cycle at the price shown on the subscribe card. You can cancel anytime from Settings → Subscription; cancellation takes effect at the end of the current paid period, and you keep Pro access until then. We email you a receipt for every renewal. If we change prices, we'll announce it in-app and honour your existing price through the current term.
terms.refundsBody:
EU / UK / Switzerland: If you're a consumer in the EU, UK, or Switzerland, you have a 14-day statutory right of withdrawal from the date of your first subscription charge. To exercise it, email gabrielandresbermudez@gmail.com within 14 days with the email address on your account — we'll issue a full refund, no questions asked.

Everywhere else: We don't offer automatic refunds for partial billing periods, but if something feels wrong — a forgotten renewal you didn't intend, a feature that didn't work for you, a platform issue on our end — email us and we'll make it right. The 7-day free trial gives you a no-charge window to confirm Macro Log is for you before any money moves.

Chargebacks: please email us before filing a chargeback; we can almost always resolve it faster than Stripe can.
terms.contactBody: Email gabrielandresbermudez@gmail.com with anything — bug reports, data-deletion requests, feature ideas, or complaints.

calculator.pageTitle: Macro calculator — calories + protein from your weight | Macro Log
calculator.title: Find your daily calories and protein.
calculator.intro: Enter your weight, pick your goal, get a target you can actually log against. No sign-up required.
calculator.weightOutOfRange: Enter a weight between {{min}} and {{max}} lb.
calculator.ctaBody: Track meals against these targets, see weekly averages, and refine as your weight changes. Free to start.
calculator.ctaFinePrint: No credit card. Pro is $3/mo or $24/yr if you want full history + AI coach.
calculator.howBody: Macro Log uses a simple bodyweight-based heuristic so you can act on a number today, before 14 days of real data:
calculator.howBullet2: Protein: weight × 1.0 g (lose), 0.9 g (maintain), or 0.8 g (gain) per pound.
calculator.howBullet3: Once you log 14+ days, Macro Log switches to a measured TDEE based on your actual weight trend.
calculator.disclaimer: Heuristic targets aren't medical advice. If you're treating a condition, talk to a registered dietitian.
calculator.faqLink: Got more questions? Read the FAQ →

title.lose: Macros to lose weight at {{weight}} lb | Macro Log
title.maintain: Macros to maintain {{weight}} lb | Macro Log
title.gain: Macros to gain weight at {{weight}} lb | Macro Log

heading.lose: Macros to lose weight at {{weight}} lb
heading.gain: Macros to gain weight at {{weight}} lb

subhead.lose: Daily calorie + protein targets for someone at {{weight}} lb who wants to drop fat.
subhead.maintain: Daily calorie + protein targets for someone at {{weight}} lb holding their current weight.
subhead.gain: Daily calorie + protein targets for someone at {{weight}} lb who wants to add mass.

explainer.lose: At {{weight}} lb, a fat-loss target lands around {{kcal}} kcal/day with {{protein}} g of protein. The protein side is high on purpose — it preserves lean mass while the calorie deficit pulls fat off. Hit the protein number first; carbs and fat fill the rest of the calorie budget.
explainer.maintain: At {{weight}} lb, a maintenance target lands around {{kcal}} kcal/day with {{protein}} g of protein. This holds your weight steady while leaving headroom for training and recovery. Track it for two weeks and adjust if your bodyweight drifts up or down.
explainer.gain: At {{weight}} lb, a lean-gain target lands around {{kcal}} kcal/day with {{protein}} g of protein. The surplus is moderate so most of the gain becomes muscle, not fat. Pair this with progressive overload in the gym 3-5×/week.

macrosPage.ctaBody: Log meals, watch your kcal + protein rings close, and refine as your weight changes.
macrosPage.disclaimer: Heuristic targets aren't medical advice. Consult a registered dietitian for any clinical condition.

faq.pageTitle: Macro & calorie FAQ — answers without the fluff | Macro Log
faq.title: Macros, calories, and fat loss — straight answers.
faq.intro: Common questions from the people who actually use Macro Log. Short, practical, no clickbait.
faq.ctaTitle: Want a target you can act on today?
faq.ctaBody: Use the free calculator. No sign-up required.

items.q: How many calories should I eat to lose weight?
items.a: A reasonable starting point is your bodyweight in pounds × 11 for fat loss. So a 180 lb person aiming to drop fat lands around 1,980 kcal/day. This works for most people because it builds in a ~500 kcal/day deficit off a typical maintenance estimate. Track for two weeks and adjust based on actual weight change — the formula is the seed, your weight trend is the truth.

items.q: How much protein do I really need?
items.a: 0.7 to 1.0 grams per pound of bodyweight. The lower end is fine for general health; the upper end matters when you're in a calorie deficit because protein protects lean mass while you lose fat. A 180 lb person targeting fat loss should aim for ~180 g/day.

items.q: Do I need to track carbs and fat?
items.a: Not strictly. If you hit your calorie ceiling and your protein floor, the carb/fat split takes care of itself for most people. Track them later if you're chasing performance gains, optimizing for satiety, or have a specific medical reason.

items.q: Why isn't the scale moving even though I'm in a deficit?
items.a: Three usual suspects: (1) you're under-counting calories — most people are off by 200-500 kcal/day, (2) water retention is masking real fat loss for 1-3 weeks, especially after a hard workout or salty meal, (3) you've been at it less than two weeks. Weight loss isn't linear; the trend over a 2-3 week window is what matters, not any single morning.

Almost never. A loss of 0.5-1% of bodyweight per week is the sweet spot — fast enough to see progress, slow enough to keep muscle and stay sane. Cutting harder than 1.5%/week tanks training quality and adherence, and the scale catches up the moment you eat normally for two days.

items.q: How accurate is a bodyweight-based calorie estimate?
items.a: Within ~10-15% for the average person. Two people at 180 lb can have maintenance calories that differ by 300+ kcal because of activity level, lean mass, and metabolic adaptation. The calculator gets you started; once you've logged 14 days, Macro Log switches to a measured TDEE based on your actual weight trend, which is much more accurate.

items.q: Do I need to weigh my food to track macros?
items.a: For the first few weeks, yes — if you want the numbers to mean anything. After 1-2 months you'll have a calibrated eyeball for common portions and can switch to estimating with reasonable accuracy. Cheap kitchen scale: $15. Best fitness investment you'll make.

A single 1,000 kcal surplus day undoes about 1.5 days of a moderate deficit — annoying but recoverable. The actual problem with cheat days is they often roll into cheat weekends. Build in 1-2 high-calorie meals per week instead of one full day off the rails. The math is identical, the psychology is gentler.

items.q: Should I eat back my exercise calories?
items.a: Mostly no. Fitness trackers over-estimate burn by 20-50%, and eating back the inflated number erases your deficit. Pick a sustainable daily calorie target and treat exercise as separate — fuel performance, build the habit, but don't use it as a calorie debit card.

items.q: Why does Macro Log not track carbs and fat?
items.a: Because for the audience we built it for — lifters, cutters, people doing recomp — kcal and protein are the two numbers that move the needle. Adding two more macros doubles the friction at log time and doesn't change outcomes for 95% of users. If you really want them, MyFitnessPal has them; we picked focus over features.

items.q: Is this a replacement for a dietitian?
items.a: No. The calculator and the app give you a starting target and a way to track adherence. If you're managing a medical condition (diabetes, kidney issues, eating disorder history, pregnancy), work with a registered dietitian. The math is general; their advice is specific to you.

items.q: What happens after I sign up?
items.a: Two-question onboarding (current weight, goal), then you're logging meals against your targets in under 30 seconds. The app stays free forever for the core flow. Pro ($3/mo) unlocks full historical charts, the AI weekly coach, and unlimited photo macro analysis.
